hero: Every Sona signal is hash-committed publicly before the market can prove it right or wrong.

Theme-exposure history was backfilled from dated filings under today's theme taxonomy — the labels benefit from hindsight; treat theme statistics as provisional until the live point-in-time record dominates.
Past performance — simulated or real — does not guarantee future results.
how_to_verify: Clone the public proof-ledger repository (this page ships inside it) — its git commit history is the independent timestamp: each commit proves the hashes it contains existed no later than the commit time.

Check the chain in ledger.jsonl: every entry's entry_hash is the SHA-256 of its canonical JSON (sorted keys, compact separators, entry_hash field removed), and each entry's prev_hash must equal the previous entry's entry_hash. Edit or remove any line and every later hash breaks.

When a prediction payload is revealed, re-hash the revealed canonical JSON file (SHA-256) and compare it to the artifact sha256 committed for that day — a match proves the prediction existed, unaltered, on the committed date.
disclaimer: Sona is informational research, not investment advice. Hypothetical and simulated results have inherent limitations: they involve no real capital, no fees, taxes, slippage or liquidity impact, and are prepared with the benefit of hindsight in their construction. Nothing here is a recommendation to buy or sell anything.
